summary: High-energy second set with soaring China>Rider, Terrapin run & epic Morning Dew
blurb: The show builds from a punchy first set featuring Jack Straw and Push, then launches into a stellar second set highlighted by a flawless China > Rider transition, an expansive Terrapin Station jam and a soaring Morning Dew finale. Jerry’s guitar is crisp and inventive throughout, while Brent’s organ adds extra funk.
review: May 2, 1987 at Frost Amphitheatre captured one of the spring ’87 tour’s high points. The first set opened with tight, high‑octane numbers like Jack Straw and When Push Comes To Shove, laying down a solid groove that set the stage for an unforgettable second set. A flawless China > Rider segue blossomed into a soaring Terrapin Station excursion, followed by a powerful drum/space segment that showcased Mickey Hart’s beam solo. The night capped with an epic Morning Dew that lifted the whole band to new heights. Jerry Garcia’s guitar was crisp and inventive, delivering standout solos on Playing In The Band, China > Rider and the climactic Dew. Brent Mydland added funky B3 organ textures, especially on Push, while Phil Lesh’s booming low end locked in tightly with the drums. Both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann drove the rhythmic pulse, making the space jams feel truly transportive. For anyone looking to relive a spring ’87 masterpiece, this show delivers high‑energy playing, memorable segues, and one of the era’s most compelling closes.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, praising the second‑set flow, China>Rider, Terrapin Station and Morning Dew; two are mixed but still highlight the same peaks while noting a few weaker moments. Consistent acclaim across multiple sources yields a moderate positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture with clear mix and full set, preserving the band’s energy while keeping crowd bleed minimal.

key_highlights: Flawless China > Rider segue into a soaring Terrapin Station jam.
Epic Morning Dew that caps an expansive second‑set space journey.
Brent Mydland’s funky B3 organ solo on “When Push Comes To Shove” adds extra drive.

band_performance.Jerry: Crisp, inventive and high‑energy playing, especially on Playing In The Band, the China > Rider transition and the soaring Morning Dew.
band_performance.Phil: Loud, clear low‑end that locks in with Jerry, providing a tight counterpoint during the China > Rider segue and driving the groove throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm work and reliable vocal delivery, including a confident slide solo on Little Red Rooster and steady backing on Lovelight.
band_performance.Brent: Funky B3 organ textures shine, highlighted by an exuberant solo on When Push Comes To Shove; the Miller upgrade adds richer keyboard depth.
band_performance.Mickey: His drum beam solo in the second set is transportive and a standout moment of the night.
band_performance.Billy: Provides a driving beat with heavy sub‑tones that propel the space jams and keep the momentum rolling.
